project.name: Position-Independent-Agent
project.description: A cross-platform remote agent written in C++23 that compiles to position-independent, zero-dependency shellcode. Implements crypto, TLS 1.3, WebSocket, HTTP, DNS, and JPEG encoding from scratch across 8 platforms and 7 architectures.

nodes.name: Common.cmake
nodes.summary: Build system orchestrator that includes all sub-modules in dependency order: logging, options, triples, compiler flags, sources, PIC transform, and post-build.

nodes.name: CompilerFlags.cmake
nodes.summary: Assembles base compiler and linker flags for freestanding C++23 builds, including architecture-specific flags for MIPS64, build-type-specific optimizations, and the pir_add_link_flags helper macro.

nodes.name: pir_add_link_flags
nodes.summary: CMake macro that appends linker flags with SHELL:-Wl, prefix to PIR_BASE_LINK_FLAGS.

nodes.name: linker.freebsd.riscv64.ld
nodes.summary: FreeBSD RISC-V 64 linker script that merges .rodata into .text with a non-zero base address (0x200000) to avoid FreeBSD's VA 0 mapping restriction.

nodes.name: linker.i386.ld
nodes.summary: i386 linker script that merges .rodata constant-pool entries into .text to maintain position-independence after LTO code generation.

nodes.name: linker.mips64.ld
nodes.summary: MIPS64 linker script that merges all sections (.rodata, .data, .bss, .got) into .text at the standard n64 ABI base address (0x120000000) for self-contained PIC binary.

nodes.name: linker.riscv64.ld
nodes.summary: RISC-V 64 linker script that merges .rodata constant-pool entries into .text for position-independent auipc+ld references.

nodes.name: linker.solaris.ld
nodes.summary: Solaris linker script with explicit PHDRS to suppress PT_PHDR and PT_GNU_STACK, which the Solaris kernel rejects for static executables without PT_INTERP.

nodes.name: Logging.cmake
nodes.summary: Unified build system logging with four levels (QUIET, STATUS, VERBOSE, DEBUG), prefixed output, component tags, and build summary helpers for key-value display and section headers.

nodes.name: pir_log_warn
nodes.summary: Emits a warning-level log message with [pir] prefix, always shown regardless of log level.

nodes.name: pir_log
nodes.summary: Emits a STATUS-level log message with [pir] prefix, shown at STATUS level and above.

nodes.name: pir_log_verbose
nodes.summary: Emits a VERBOSE-level log message with [pir] prefix, shown at VERBOSE level and above.

nodes.name: pir_log_debug
nodes.summary: Emits a DEBUG-level log message with [pir] prefix, shown only at DEBUG log level.

nodes.name: pir_log_header
nodes.summary: Prints a decorated section header for build summary output.

nodes.name: pir_log_kv
nodes.summary: Prints a key-value pair with dot-leader alignment for build summary output.

nodes.name: pir_log_list
nodes.summary: Prints a labeled list of items at VERBOSE log level with count and bullet formatting.

nodes.name: Options.cmake
nodes.summary: Defines and validates build options (ARCHITECTURE, PLATFORM, BUILD_TYPE, OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL, BUILD_TESTS, ENABLE_LOGGING), normalizes inputs, and derives preprocessor defines and output paths.

nodes.name: PICTransform.cmake
nodes.summary: Acquires the pic-transform LLVM pass tool by searching PATH or building from in-tree source, producing either a loadable plugin (.so/.dylib/.dll) or standalone executable for eliminating data sections.

nodes.name: _pt_found
nodes.summary: Helper macro that sets PIC_TRANSFORM_PLUGIN or PIC_TRANSFORM_EXECUTABLE output variables and returns from the module.

nodes.name: _pt_find_artifact
nodes.summary: Helper macro that searches for a built artifact in both single-config and multi-config (Release/) build layouts.

nodes.name: Android.cmake
nodes.summary: Android platform configuration that reuses Linux syscalls, adds ARMv7a hardware FP flags, and configures ELF linker settings with LLD.

nodes.name: FreeBSD.cmake
nodes.summary: FreeBSD platform configuration supporting i386, x86_64, aarch64, and riscv64 with ELF linker settings, --pie for x86, custom linker scripts for RISC-V, and ELFOSABI patching to ELFOSABI_FREEBSD.

nodes.name: iOS.cmake
nodes.summary: iOS platform configuration (aarch64 only) that reuses XNU syscalls, configures Mach-O linker settings with dyld support, and forces entry_point.cc without LTO for correct section ordering.

nodes.name: Linux.cmake
nodes.summary: Linux platform configuration with ELF linker settings, custom linker scripts for i386/riscv64/mips64 to merge .rodata into .text, and LLD integration.

nodes.name: macOS.cmake
nodes.summary: macOS platform configuration for x86_64 and aarch64 with Mach-O linker settings, static linking for x86_64, dyld-based linking for ARM64, and entry_point.cc LTO workaround for correct section ordering.

nodes.name: Solaris.cmake
nodes.summary: Solaris/illumos platform configuration that invokes ld.lld directly (bypassing Clang driver's -C injection), uses custom linker script for PT_PHDR suppression, and patches ELFOSABI to ELFOSABI_SOLARIS.

nodes.name: UEFI.cmake
nodes.summary: UEFI platform configuration for x86_64 and aarch64 with PE/COFF linker settings, EFI_APPLICATION subsystem, and a pir_add_uefi_boot function to create EFI boot images.

nodes.name: pir_add_uefi_boot
nodes.summary: Creates post-build commands to copy the output binary into the EFI/BOOT directory structure with the correct architecture-specific filename (BOOTX64.EFI or BOOTAA64.EFI).

nodes.name: Windows.cmake
nodes.summary: Windows platform configuration with PE/COFF linker settings, CodeView debug info for PDB generation, WSL path remapping, and architecture-specific flags.

nodes.name: PostBuild.cmake
nodes.summary: Defines the pir_add_postbuild function that orchestrates the post-build pipeline: optional ELF OSABI patching, .text section extraction, base64 encoding, and PIC mode verification.

nodes.name: pir_add_postbuild
nodes.summary: Registers post-build commands for a CMake target: OSABI patching, binary extraction, base64 encoding, and PIC verification; skips PIC artifacts for debug optimization levels.

nodes.name: Base64Encode.cmake
nodes.summary: Cross-platform CMake script (run via cmake -P) that base64-encodes a PIC binary file using certutil on Windows or base64 on Unix (handling both GNU and BSD variants).

nodes.name: ExtractBinary.cmake
nodes.summary: CMake script that extracts the .text/__TEXT,__text section from ELF/PE/Mach-O binaries using llvm-objcopy, generates disassembly, extracts strings, and reports file sizes and extraction ratios.

nodes.name: PatchElfOSABI.cmake
nodes.summary: CMake script that patches byte 7 (EI_OSABI) of an ELF header in-place using PowerShell on Windows or printf+dd on Unix, enabling Solaris/FreeBSD kernel compatibility.

nodes.name: VerifyPICMode.cmake
nodes.summary: CMake script that verifies position-independence by checking the linker map file: ensures entry_point is the first function in .text and no forbidden data sections (.rdata, .rodata, .data, .bss, .got, .plt, __DATA) exist.

nodes.name: Sources.cmake
nodes.summary: Auto-discovers source files and include paths from src/ directory tree, appends application layer sources (beacon or tests), and provides the pir_filter_sources macro for platform-based source exclusion.

nodes.name: pir_filter_sources
nodes.summary: CMake macro that excludes sources and include paths for listed platforms by applying regex filters on PIR_SOURCES and PIR_INCLUDE_PATHS.

nodes.name: Target.cmake
nodes.summary: Loads the platform-specific CMake module, creates the executable target with all flags/defines/includes, integrates pic-transform (plugin or standalone mode), invokes post-build steps, and prints the build summary.

nodes.name: Toolchain.cmake
nodes.summary: Freestanding CMake toolchain file that sets clang/clang++ as the compiler, skips compiler tests, clears standard libraries, and defines a minimal link command.

nodes.name: Triples.cmake
nodes.summary: Maps all platform/architecture combinations to LLVM target triples and file extensions, and provides the pir_get_target_info macro to resolve them.

nodes.name: pir_get_target_info
nodes.summary: CMake macro that resolves PIR_TRIPLE and PIR_EXT from the current PIR_PLATFORM and PIR_ARCH combination.

nodes.name: loader.py
nodes.summary: Cross-platform Python PIC shellcode loader that auto-detects host OS/architecture, downloads shellcode from GitHub Releases or loads from a local file, and executes via mmap+mprotect on POSIX or process injection on Windows.

nodes.name: _log
nodes.summary: Timestamped log function with level filtering and immediate stdout flush.

nodes.name: _detect_os
nodes.summary: Detects the host OS, distinguishing iOS and Android from their parent kernels (Darwin and Linux).

nodes.name: _detect_arch
nodes.summary: Detects CPU family and bitness from platform.machine(), including iOS device model identifiers.

nodes.name: get_host
nodes.summary: Returns (os_name, family, bits) tuple for the current host by combining _detect_os and _detect_arch.

nodes.name: download
nodes.summary: Downloads PIC shellcode from GitHub Releases for a given platform/arch/tag, validates the payload is not HTML and meets minimum size requirements.

nodes.name: run_mmap
nodes.summary: POSIX shellcode execution: allocates RW memory via mmap, copies shellcode, flips to RX via mprotect, flushes instruction cache on ARM64 Darwin, and invokes as a C function pointer.

nodes.name: setup_kernel32
nodes.summary: Configures ctypes argtypes/restype for Windows kernel32 API functions used in process injection (VirtualAllocEx, WriteProcessMemory, CreateRemoteThread, etc.).

nodes.name: run_injected
nodes.summary: Windows process injection: creates a suspended process, allocates RW memory, writes shellcode, flips to RX, creates a remote thread, and waits for completion; supports cross-family injection via PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_MACHINE_TYPE.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI arguments, detects host environment, loads shellcode from local file or GitHub Releases, and dispatches to run_mmap (POSIX) or run_injected (Windows).

nodes.name: loader.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell PIC payload loader for Windows that auto-detects architecture, downloads shellcode from GitHub Releases, and executes in-process via VirtualAlloc/VirtualProtect/delegate invocation.

nodes.name: Write-Log
nodes.summary: Color-coded timestamped logging function with level prefix support (DBG, INF, OK, WRN, ERR).

nodes.name: Get-HostArch
nodes.summary: Detects host architecture from PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE environment variable, mapping to project architecture names (x86_64, i386, aarch64, armv7a).

nodes.name: Get-Payload
nodes.summary: Downloads PIC payload from GitHub Releases using WebClient, with SSL verification bypass and payload validation (size check, HTML detection).

nodes.name: Invoke-Payload
nodes.summary: Executes shellcode in-process: allocates RW memory with VirtualAlloc, copies data, flips to RX with VirtualProtect, and invokes as a delegate function pointer.

nodes.name: Main
nodes.summary: Entry point that detects host architecture, downloads payload, and invokes it via Invoke-Payload.

nodes.name: commands.h
nodes.summary: Header defining the beacon command protocol: CommandType enum (system info, directory listing, file content, shell I/O, screenshots), StatusCode enum, Context struct for shell/screen capture state, and CommandHandler function pointer type.

nodes.name: Context
nodes.summary: Holds runtime state for command handlers including Shell and ScreenCaptureContext pointers, with a destructor that cleans up both resources.

nodes.name: commandsHandler.cc
nodes.summary: Implements command handlers for the beacon agent including file operations (read, hash, directory listing), shell I/O, display enumeration, and screenshot capture with JPEG encoding and dirty-rectangle diffing.

nodes.name: main.cc
nodes.summary: Entry point for the beacon agent; establishes a WebSocket connection to the relay server, dispatches incoming command messages to registered handlers, and manages reconnection logic.

nodes.name: screen_capture.h
nodes.summary: Defines data structures for screen capture operations including JpegBuffer, Graphics (per-display frame buffers and diff state), GraphicsList, ScreenCaptureContext, and Rectangle for JPEG-encoded screen regions.

nodes.name: shell.cc
nodes.summary: Implements the Shell class which wraps ShellProcess to provide Create, Write, Read, and ReadError operations with poll-based reading and prompt detection.

nodes.name: shell.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Shell class interface providing factory creation, write, read, and read-error methods over a wrapped ShellProcess with move-only semantics.

nodes.name: base64.cc
nodes.summary: Implements RFC 4648 Base64 encoding and decoding using branchless arithmetic character mapping to avoid lookup tables in .rdata, ensuring position-independence.

nodes.name: base64.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Base64 static class with Encode, Decode, GetEncodeOutSize, and GetDecodeOutSize methods for RFC 4648 Base64 operations without CRT dependencies.

nodes.name: djb2.h
nodes.summary: Implements the DJB2 hash algorithm with build-unique FNV-1a seeding from __DATE__, providing case-insensitive runtime and compile-time hashing for PE export name resolution and anti-signature detection.

nodes.name: binary_reader.h
nodes.summary: Provides a sequential, bounds-checked binary data reader with big-endian integer deserialization (16/24/32-bit), used for parsing network protocol messages and TLS records.

nodes.name: binary_writer.h
nodes.summary: Provides a sequential, bounds-checked binary data writer with big-endian integer serialization (8/16/24/32-bit), used for constructing network protocol messages and TLS records.

nodes.name: compiler_builtins.h
nodes.summary: Provides position-independent software replacements for __builtin_clzll and __builtin_ctzll on RISC-V 32-bit targets, avoiding De Bruijn lookup tables that would break position-independence.

nodes.name: compiler.h
nodes.summary: Defines compiler-specific macros for Clang/LLVM including FORCE_INLINE, NOINLINE, DISABLE_OPTIMIZATION, COMPILER_RUNTIME, NO_RETURN, and ENTRYPOINT with platform-specific stack alignment handling.

nodes.name: compiler_runtime.armv7a.cc
nodes.summary: Implements ARM EABI compiler runtime functions for ARMv7-A: 32/64-bit division and modulo, 64-bit shifts, IEEE-754 float conversions, and memory operations required by -nostdlib builds.

nodes.name: compiler_runtime.i386.cc
nodes.summary: Implements x86 (i386) compiler runtime functions for 64-bit division, modulo, and shift operations required when building 32-bit x86 code with -nostdlib.

nodes.name: compiler_runtime.riscv32.cc
nodes.summary: Implements RISC-V 32-bit compiler runtime functions for 64-bit division, modulo, shifts, and IEEE-754 float conversions required when building RV32 code with -nostdlib.

nodes.name: compiler_runtime.windows.armv7a.cc
nodes.summary: Implements Windows ARM-specific compiler runtime functions (__rt_udiv, __rt_udiv64, float conversions) with the reversed argument ordering required by the Windows ARM calling convention.

nodes.name: core.h
nodes.summary: Umbrella header for the Core Abstraction Layer that includes all core subsystems: compiler macros, memory, math, types, strings, algorithms, encoding, network types, and binary I/O.

nodes.name: utf16.h
nodes.summary: Provides platform-independent UTF-16 to UTF-8 conversion with full surrogate pair handling for characters beyond the Basic Multilingual Plane, used for Windows wide string interoperability.

nodes.name: bitops.h
nodes.summary: Provides templated bit rotation operations (Rotr/Rotl for 32 and 64-bit) used by SHA-2, ChaCha20, and other cryptographic primitives, compiling to single CPU instructions on most architectures.

nodes.name: byteorder.h
nodes.summary: Provides byte order swapping (Swap16/32/64) via compiler intrinsics for network-to-host and host-to-network conversions, compiling to BSWAP/REV instructions.

nodes.name: math.h
nodes.summary: Provides static template math utilities (Min, Max, Abs, Clamp) with common-type deduction and force-inlining, all CRT-free and position-independent.

nodes.name: prng.h
nodes.summary: Implements a xorshift64 pseudorandom number generator with methods for integer generation, byte array filling, and random character/string generation.

nodes.name: memory.cc
nodes.summary: Implements CRT-free memory operations (memset, memcpy, memmove, memcmp, bzero) conforming to C17 semantics with word-at-a-time optimization for aligned regions.

nodes.name: memory.h
nodes.summary: Declares CRT-free C memory functions (memset, memcpy, memmove, memcmp, bzero) and the Memory wrapper class providing Copy, Zero, Set, Move, and Compare as force-inlined static methods.

nodes.name: string.cc
nodes.summary: Implements non-inline StringUtils functions: FloatToStr (double to string), StrToFloat (string to double), and Utf8ToWide (UTF-8 to wide string with surrogate pair support).

nodes.name: string_formatter.h
nodes.summary: Implements a printf-style string formatter with callback-based output, supporting format specifiers for integers, hex, floats, strings, wide strings, pointers, and Error structs, all without CRT dependencies.

nodes.name: string.h
nodes.summary: Declares and implements the StringUtils static class with comprehensive string operations: character classification, case conversion, comparison, searching, copying, number-to-string and string-to-number conversion, and UTF encoding.

nodes.name: error.h
nodes.summary: Defines the unified Error struct with a fixed-capacity cause chain supporting Runtime, Windows (NTSTATUS), Posix (errno), and UEFI (EFI_STATUS) platform error codes, used by Result<T, Error> throughout the codebase.

nodes.name: ip_address.cc
nodes.summary: Implements IPAddress parsing from string (IPv4 dotted-decimal and IPv6 colon-hex with double-colon expansion) and conversion to string representation.

nodes.name: ip_address.h
nodes.summary: Declares the IPAddress class supporting unified IPv4/IPv6 representation with constexpr construction, factory methods, string parsing/formatting, comparison operators, and loopback address helpers.

nodes.name: Handle_GetSystemInfoCommand
nodes.summary: Retrieves system information and agent build info, serializes them into a response buffer.

nodes.name: Handle_GetDirectoryContentCommand
nodes.summary: Lists directory entries using DirectoryIterator, serializes them as WireDirectoryEntry structs in the response.

nodes.name: Handle_GetFileContentCommand
nodes.summary: Reads a chunk of file content at a given offset and returns it in the response buffer.

nodes.name: Handle_GetFileChunkHashCommand
nodes.summary: Computes and returns a SHA-256 hash of a file chunk by reading in small blocks and updating the digest.

nodes.name: Handle_WriteShellCommand
nodes.summary: Writes a command string to the agent's shell process, creating the shell if needed.

nodes.name: Handle_ReadShellCommand
nodes.summary: Reads output from the agent's shell process stdout into the response buffer.

nodes.name: Handle_ResetShellCommand
nodes.summary: Resets the agent's shell process by destroying the existing Shell instance.

nodes.name: Handle_GetDisplaysCommand
nodes.summary: Enumerates display devices and returns them as a serialized ScreenDevice array.

nodes.name: Handle_GetScreenshotCommand
nodes.summary: Captures a screenshot of a display, computing dirty rectangles for incremental updates or encoding a full-screen JPEG, and returns the compressed image segments.

nodes.name: start
nodes.summary: Agent entry point that registers command handlers, connects via WebSocket, and runs the message receive/dispatch/response loop with reconnection.

nodes.name: JpegBuffer
nodes.summary: Manages a reusable dynamically-allocated JPEG output buffer with reset and auto-cleanup.

nodes.name: Graphics
nodes.summary: Holds per-display screenshot buffers (current frame, previous frame, bidiff, rect buffer, and JPEG buffer) for incremental screen capture.

nodes.name: ScreenCaptureContext
nodes.summary: Top-level context holding the display device list and graphics array for multi-display screen capture sessions.

nodes.name: Shell
nodes.summary: Move-only shell wrapper providing factory Create(), Write(), Read(), and ReadError() over ShellProcess.

nodes.name: Base64
nodes.summary: Static class for RFC 4648 Base64 encoding/decoding with compile-time size computation helpers.

nodes.name: Djb2
nodes.summary: DJB2 hash with build-unique FNV-1a seed providing runtime Hash(), Span-based Hash(), and consteval HashCompileTime() for API name resolution.

nodes.name: BinaryReader
nodes.summary: Sequential bounds-checked reader for binary data with big-endian multi-byte integer deserialization and cursor control.

nodes.name: BinaryWriter
nodes.summary: Sequential bounds-checked writer for binary data with big-endian multi-byte integer serialization and cursor control.

nodes.name: UTF16
nodes.summary: Static class for UTF-16 to UTF-8 conversion with surrogate pair handling.

nodes.name: BitOps
nodes.summary: Static class providing templated bit rotation (Rotr/Rotl) for 32 and 64-bit values.

nodes.name: ByteOrder
nodes.summary: Static class providing Swap16/Swap32/Swap64 byte order conversion via compiler intrinsics.

nodes.name: Math
nodes.summary: Static template math utilities: Min, Max, Abs, Clamp.

nodes.name: Prng
nodes.summary: Xorshift64 PRNG with seed, Get, GetArray, GetChar, and GetString methods.

nodes.name: Memory
nodes.summary: C++ wrapper class providing force-inlined static methods (Copy, Zero, Set, Move, Compare) over CRT-free memory functions.

nodes.name: StringUtils
nodes.summary: Comprehensive static string utility class with character classification, case conversion, comparison, searching, copying, number conversion, and UTF encoding support.

nodes.name: StringFormatter
nodes.summary: Printf-style formatter with callback-based output, type-erased Argument system, and support for integers, hex, floats, strings, pointers, and Error structs.

nodes.name: Error
nodes.summary: Unified error struct with fixed-capacity cause chain (up to 5 levels) supporting Runtime, Windows, Posix, and UEFI platform error codes.

nodes.name: IPAddress
nodes.summary: Unified IPv4/IPv6 address class with constexpr construction, factory methods, string parsing/formatting, and comparison operators.

nodes.name: memset
nodes.summary: CRT-free memset with word-at-a-time optimization for aligned regions.

nodes.name: memcpy
nodes.summary: CRT-free memcpy with word-at-a-time optimization when both pointers are aligned.

nodes.name: memmove
nodes.summary: CRT-free memmove handling overlapping regions with forward/backward copy direction selection.

nodes.name: StringUtils::FloatToStr
nodes.summary: Converts a double to string with configurable precision and trailing zero trimming.

nodes.name: StringUtils::StrToFloat
nodes.summary: Parses a string span into a double, handling sign, integer, and fractional parts.

nodes.name: StringUtils::Utf8ToWide
nodes.summary: Converts a UTF-8 string to wide string with 4-byte codepoint and UTF-16 surrogate pair handling.

nodes.name: IPAddress::FromString
nodes.summary: Parses an IP address from a string, supporting both IPv4 dotted-decimal and IPv6 colon-hex notation with double-colon expansion.

nodes.name: IPAddress::ToString
nodes.summary: Converts an IPAddress to its string representation (dotted-decimal for IPv4, colon-hex for IPv6).

nodes.name: point.h
nodes.summary: Defines a 2D Point struct with Row/Col integer coordinates, used by contour detection and image processing.

nodes.name: primitives.h
nodes.summary: Defines fundamental fixed-width integer types (INT8-UINT64), character types, boolean, size types, variadic argument macros, platform-specific calling conventions, and floating-point types used throughout the entire codebase.

nodes.name: result.h
nodes.summary: Implements a zero-cost tagged union Result<T, E> type for error handling with Ok/Err factories, move semantics, error chaining via Wrap(), and a compact specialization for Result<void, Error>.

nodes.name: Result<T, E>
nodes.summary: Tagged union holding either a success value T or error E, with [[nodiscard]] enforcement, move-only semantics, and multi-arg error chaining via Wrap().

nodes.name: rgb.h
nodes.summary: Defines the RGB pixel struct with 8-bit Red, Green, Blue channels, used by display capture, image processing, and JPEG encoding.

nodes.name: span.h
nodes.summary: Provides non-owning contiguous buffer views (Span<T> dynamic extent and Span<T,N> static extent) with zero-cost element access, slicing, implicit const/extent conversions, and stack-only allocation.

nodes.name: Span<T, Extent>
nodes.summary: Non-owning view over contiguous memory with both dynamic-extent (pointer+size) and static-extent (pointer-only) specializations, modeled after C++20 std::span without STL dependencies.

nodes.name: uuid.h
nodes.summary: Implements 128-bit UUID generation, parsing from 8-4-4-4-12 hex strings, and serialization to string format per RFC 9562.

nodes.name: UUID
nodes.summary: 128-bit UUID stored as 16-byte array with FromString parsing, ToString serialization, and MSB/LSB accessors.

nodes.name: entry_point.cc
nodes.summary: Unified entry point for all platforms (UEFI and native), initializes platform-specific context (EFI handles on UEFI), calls start(), and terminates via ExitProcess.

nodes.name: entry_point
nodes.summary: Platform-specific entry point that sets up UEFI context if needed, invokes start(), and calls ExitProcess with the exit code.

nodes.name: chacha20.cc
nodes.summary: Implements the ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD cipher per RFC 8439, including the Poly1305 MAC (ProcessBlocks, Update, Finish, GenerateKey), ChaCha20 stream cipher (KeySetup, EncryptBytes, Block), and combined AEAD operations (Poly1305Aead, Poly1305Decode).

nodes.name: Poly1305
nodes.summary: Poly1305 one-time message authentication code with 256-bit key, supporting incremental Update/Finish API and static key generation from ChaCha20 keystream.

nodes.name: ChaCha20Poly1305
nodes.summary: ChaCha20-Poly1305 authenticated encryption combining ChaCha20 stream cipher with Poly1305 MAC, supporting 256-bit keys, 96-bit nonces, and AEAD encode/decode operations.

nodes.name: chacha20_encoder.cc
nodes.summary: Implements the TLS 1.3 ChaCha20-Poly1305 record layer encoder with bidirectional encryption/decryption, per-record nonce derivation, and AEAD authentication.

nodes.name: chacha20_encoder.h
nodes.summary: Declares ChaCha20Encoder class providing bidirectional TLS 1.3 record encryption/decryption with separate cipher states and nonces for local and remote traffic.

nodes.name: ChaCha20Encoder
nodes.summary: Bidirectional TLS 1.3 record encryption/decryption managing separate ChaCha20-Poly1305 cipher states for send and receive directions with automatic nonce management.

nodes.name: chacha20.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Poly1305 MAC and ChaCha20Poly1305 AEAD cipher classes per RFC 8439, with constants for block/key/tag sizes and TLS 1.3 IV length.

nodes.name: ecc.cc
nodes.summary: Implements elliptic curve cryptography for NIST P-256 and P-384 curves, including variable-length integer arithmetic, modular operations, point multiplication, key generation, and ECDH shared secret computation.

nodes.name: ecc.h
nodes.summary: Declares the ECC class for Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange supporting NIST P-256 and P-384 curves, with VLI arithmetic, point operations, and key serialization methods.

nodes.name: ECC
nodes.summary: Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange implementing NIST P-256/P-384 curves with ephemeral key pair generation, public key export, and shared secret computation.

nodes.name: sha2.cc
nodes.summary: Implements SHA-256 and SHA-384 hash functions and their HMAC counterparts using traits-based templates, with constants embedded in code to avoid .rdata dependencies.

nodes.name: sha2.h
nodes.summary: Declares SHA-256/SHA-384 hash functions and HMAC-SHA256/HMAC-SHA384 message authentication codes via traits-based SHABase and HMACBase template classes.

nodes.name: SHABase<Traits>
nodes.summary: Traits-parametrized SHA-2 hash implementation supporting incremental Update/Final and one-shot Hash, with Merkle-Damgard construction for both SHA-256 and SHA-384.

nodes.name: HMACBase<SHAType, Traits>
nodes.summary: Traits-parametrized HMAC implementation per RFC 2104 supporting Init/Update/Final and one-shot Compute, with efficient Reinit for same-key reuse.

nodes.name: image_processor.cc
nodes.summary: Implements binary image differencing (with optional SAD threshold) and tile-based dirty region detection with greedy row-span merging for real-time screen streaming.

nodes.name: image_processor.h
nodes.summary: Declares ImageProcessor with static methods for per-pixel binary differencing and tile-based dirty rectangle detection, plus DirtyRect/DirtyRectResult structs.

nodes.name: ImageProcessor
nodes.summary: Stateless utility class providing static methods for RGB image differencing (CalculateBiDifference) and dirty rectangle detection (FindDirtyRects).

nodes.name: jpeg_encoder.cc
nodes.summary: Implements baseline JPEG encoding (ITU-T T.81) using the Arai-Agui-Nakajima scaled DCT algorithm with float constants embedded as UINT32 immediates to maintain position-independence.

nodes.name: jpeg_encoder.h
nodes.summary: Declares JpegEncoder class with a static Encode method for baseline JPEG compression of RGB/RGBA images, streaming output through a user-provided write callback.

nodes.name: JpegEncoder
nodes.summary: Stateless baseline JPEG encoder with a single static Encode method supporting quality 1-100, RGB/RGBA input, and callback-based output streaming.

nodes.name: dns_client.cc
nodes.summary: Implements DNS-over-HTTPS resolver sending RFC 1035 wire-format queries via TLS 1.3 HTTP POST to Cloudflare and Google DoH servers, with automatic IPv6-to-IPv4 fallback.

nodes.name: dns_client.h
nodes.summary: Declares DnsClient class for DNS-over-HTTPS hostname resolution via Cloudflare and Google DoH servers, with A/AAAA record type support and multi-provider fallback.

nodes.name: DnsClient
nodes.summary: DNS-over-HTTPS stub resolver querying Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) and Google (8.8.8.8) with automatic provider and IPv6-to-IPv4 fallback.

nodes.name: http_client.cc
nodes.summary: Implements HttpClient with URL parsing (http/https/ws/wss), DNS resolution, TLS connection creation with IPv6-to-IPv4 fallback, GET/POST request building, and rolling-window HTTP response header parsing.

nodes.name: http_client.h
nodes.summary: Declares HttpClient class for HTTP/1.1 requests over plaintext or TLS 1.3 connections, with factory Create method, URL parsing, GET/POST requests, and response header parsing.

nodes.name: HttpClient
nodes.summary: HTTP/1.1 client with TLS 1.3 support, factory-based construction from URLs, DNS resolution, GET/POST requests, and rolling-window header parsing.

nodes.name: tls_buffer.cc
nodes.summary: Implements TlsBuffer's dynamic memory management: append, resize with 4x growth, consume (shift), read, and 24-bit big-endian integer reading for TLS record parsing.

nodes.name: tls_buffer.h
nodes.summary: Declares TlsBuffer class, a unified read/write buffer with dynamic resizing, move semantics, typed read/write templates, and consume (shift-down) operations for TLS record processing.

nodes.name: TlsBuffer
nodes.summary: Dynamically resizable byte buffer for TLS record I/O with typed append/read, 24-bit BE reading, consume-and-shift, and ownership tracking.

nodes.name: tls_cipher.cc
nodes.summary: Implements TlsCipher's TLS 1.3 key schedule: HKDF-based key derivation (ComputeKey), ECC public key computation, pre-master key generation, verify data computation, and ChaCha20-Poly1305 record encode/decode with AAD and sequence numbers.

nodes.name: tls_cipher.h
nodes.summary: Declares TlsCipher class managing TLS 1.3 cipher state including ECC key pairs, ChaCha20Encoder, handshake hash, key derivation, and record-level encode/decode operations.

nodes.name: TlsCipher
nodes.summary: TLS 1.3 cipher state manager handling ECC key pairs, HKDF key schedule, handshake hashing, ChaCha20-Poly1305 encode/decode, and sequence number tracking.

nodes.name: tls_client.cc
nodes.summary: Implements TLS 1.3 client handshake protocol including ClientHello/ServerHello exchange, key share negotiation, encrypted extensions processing, certificate verification, and bidirectional record-layer read/write over sockets.

nodes.name: tls_client.h
nodes.summary: Declares TlsClient class providing TLS 1.3 and plaintext TCP connections with factory-based creation, handshake state machine, and buffered read/write operations.

nodes.name: TlsClient
nodes.summary: TLS 1.3 client with factory Create method, full handshake state machine, ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption, and buffered bidirectional read/write over raw sockets.

nodes.name: tls_hash.cc
nodes.summary: Implements TlsHash methods for caching handshake data and computing SHA-256 or SHA-384 hashes on demand based on output span size.

nodes.name: tls_hash.h
nodes.summary: Declares the TlsHash class that wraps a TlsBuffer cache for incremental hashing, supporting SHA-256 and SHA-384 dispatch based on output size. Non-copyable, move-only, stack-only type with placement new for Result compatibility.

nodes.name: TlsHash
nodes.summary: Incremental hash accumulator for TLS handshake transcript hashing; caches data via TlsBuffer and dispatches to SHA-256 or SHA-384 based on the requested output size.

nodes.name: tls_hkdf.cc
nodes.summary: Implements the TLS 1.3 HKDF key derivation functions: Label construction, Extract (HMAC-SHA256 PRK extraction), Expand (iterative HMAC expansion), and ExpandLabel (TLS 1.3 labeled expansion).

nodes.name: TlsHKDF::Label
nodes.summary: Creates an HKDF label structure per TLS 1.3 specification, serializing the 'tls13 ' prefix, label, data, and output length into a binary buffer using BinaryWriter.

nodes.name: TlsHKDF::Extract
nodes.summary: Performs HKDF-Extract using HMAC-SHA256 to derive a pseudorandom key (PRK) from a salt and input keying material.

nodes.name: TlsHKDF::Expand
nodes.summary: Performs HKDF-Expand using iterative HMAC-SHA256 to produce output keying material of arbitrary length from a secret and info parameter.

nodes.name: TlsHKDF::ExpandLabel
nodes.summary: Combines Label construction and Expand to perform TLS 1.3 HKDF-Expand-Label, the primary key derivation primitive used in the TLS 1.3 key schedule.

nodes.name: tls_hkdf.h
nodes.summary: Declares the TlsHKDF static class providing HKDF Extract, Expand, and ExpandLabel operations for TLS 1.3 key derivation.

nodes.name: TlsHKDF
nodes.summary: Static utility class implementing the HMAC-based Key Derivation Function (HKDF) for TLS 1.3, with Extract, Expand, and ExpandLabel methods.

nodes.name: websocket_client.cc
nodes.summary: Implements the WebSocket client per RFC 6455: opening handshake with HTTP Upgrade, masked frame construction/sending, frame parsing with variable-length payloads, message reassembly with fragmentation support, control frame handling (Close/Ping/Pong), and a factory Create method with DNS resolution and IPv4/IPv6 fallback.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient::Open
nodes.summary: Performs the WebSocket opening handshake (RFC 6455 Section 4): opens TLS transport with IPv4 fallback, generates Base64-encoded Sec-WebSocket-Key, sends HTTP Upgrade request, and validates HTTP 101 response.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient::Close
nodes.summary: Sends a Close frame with status 1000 (Normal Closure) in network byte order and tears down the TLS transport per RFC 6455 Section 7.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient::Write
nodes.summary: Constructs and sends a masked WebSocket frame with FIN=1, variable-length encoding (7/16/64-bit), random masking key, and coalesced or chunked TLS writes for small vs large payloads.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient::ReceiveFrame
nodes.summary: Reads and parses a single WebSocket frame from the wire: 2-byte header, optional extended length, optional mask key, payload allocation and read, with RSV validation and 64MB size limit.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient::Read
nodes.summary: Reads the next complete WebSocket message with fragmentation reassembly (RFC 6455 Section 5.4), handling interleaved Close/Ping/Pong control frames transparently.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient::Create
nodes.summary: Factory method that parses a ws/wss URL, resolves DNS (with AAAA-to-A fallback), creates TLS transport, and performs the opening handshake to return a connected WebSocketClient.

nodes.name: websocket_client.h
nodes.summary: Declares the WebSocket protocol client (RFC 6455) including WebSocketOpcode enum, WebSocketFrame and WebSocketMessage structs with move semantics, and the WebSocketClient class with factory creation, read/write, and close operations over TLS transport.

nodes.name: WebSocketClient
nodes.summary: Full WebSocket client implementing RFC 6455 over TLS/plaintext transport: opening handshake, masked framing, fragmentation reassembly, control frame handling, and closing handshake. Stack-only, move-only type.

nodes.name: WebSocketFrame
nodes.summary: Represents a single parsed WebSocket frame with FIN, RSV, opcode, mask, payload length, and heap-allocated data. Move-only with RAII cleanup.

nodes.name: WebSocketMessage
nodes.summary: Represents a fully reassembled WebSocket message from potentially multiple fragments, owning its concatenated payload buffer. Move-only with RAII cleanup.

nodes.name: runtime.h

nodes.name: vector.h
nodes.summary: Implements a generic owning dynamic array (Vector<T>) with RAII, move-only semantics, exponential growth reallocation, fallible Init/Add, and stack-only enforcement via deleted heap operators.

nodes.name: Vector<T>
nodes.summary: Template dynamic array with exponential growth (2x), fallible allocation (Init/Add return BOOL), move-only ownership, and Release() to transfer buffer ownership to the caller.

nodes.name: console.cc
nodes.summary: Implements Console::Write for wide-character (WCHAR) output on non-UEFI platforms by converting UTF-16 to UTF-8 in 256-byte stack-based chunks before writing.

nodes.name: Console::Write(Span<const WCHAR>)
nodes.summary: Converts wide-character text to UTF-8 in stack-buffered 256-byte chunks using UTF16::CodepointToUTF8, then delegates to the narrow Console::Write.

nodes.name: console.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Console static class providing position-independent printf-style console output without CRT dependencies, supporting both narrow (CHAR) and wide (WCHAR) character types via direct syscalls and variadic template formatting.

nodes.name: Console
nodes.summary: Static console I/O class with platform-specific Write implementations, null-terminated string convenience wrappers, and variadic template WriteFormatted using StringFormatter with character-by-character callback emission.

nodes.name: logger.h
nodes.summary: Provides structured logging with ANSI color support, log level filtering (Info/Error/Warning/Debug), timestamped output, and zero-overhead compile-time disabling via ENABLE_LOGGING/ENABLE_DEBUG_LOGGING preprocessor flags.

nodes.name: Logger
nodes.summary: Static logging utility with ANSI-colored level tags, HH:MM:SS timestamps, and careful code-gen design to avoid stack-slot colouring, tail-call, and PIC pointer escape hazards on Windows aarch64 -Oz.

nodes.name: posix/console.cc
nodes.summary: POSIX implementation of Console::Write for narrow strings, using the write() syscall directly to stdout across Linux, Android, macOS, iOS, Solaris, and FreeBSD.

nodes.name: uefi/console.cc
nodes.summary: UEFI implementation of Console::Write for both WCHAR and CHAR, outputting through EFI_SIMPLE_TEXT_OUTPUT_PROTOCOL with chunk-based CHAR16 conversion and null termination.

nodes.name: windows/console.cc
nodes.summary: Windows implementation of Console::Write for narrow strings, using NtDll ZwWriteFile via the PEB's StandardOutput handle.

nodes.name: directory_entry.h

nodes.name: DirectoryEntry

nodes.name: directory.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Directory static class with Create and Delete methods returning Result<void, Error> for cross-platform directory operations via NtCreateFile, mkdir syscall, or EFI_FILE_PROTOCOL.

nodes.name: Directory
nodes.summary: Static class providing platform-independent directory Create and Delete operations using Result-based error handling.

nodes.name: directory_iterator.h

nodes.name: DirectoryIterator

nodes.name: file.h

nodes.name: File

nodes.name: offset_origin.h

nodes.name: path.h

nodes.name: Path
nodes.summary: Static utility class with constexpr template methods for path combining, filename/extension/directory extraction, root detection, and separator normalization across Windows/UEFI/POSIX.

nodes.name: posix/directory.cc
nodes.summary: POSIX implementation of Directory::Create and Directory::Delete using mkdir/mkdirat and rmdir/unlinkat syscalls with architecture-specific dispatch (aarch64/riscv use *at variants).

nodes.name: Directory::Create [POSIX]
nodes.summary: Creates a directory with mode 0755 using mkdir or mkdirat syscall depending on architecture, converting wide path to UTF-8 first.

nodes.name: Directory::Delete [POSIX]
nodes.summary: Deletes a directory using rmdir or unlinkat syscall depending on architecture, converting wide path to UTF-8 first.

nodes.name: posix/directory_iterator.cc
nodes.summary: POSIX implementation of DirectoryIterator using getdents64/getdirentries64 with 1KB kernel buffer, architecture-specific stat field offset tables for extracting file metadata (mode, size, mtime) across Linux, macOS, iOS, Solaris, and FreeBSD.

nodes.name: DirectoryIterator::Create [POSIX]
nodes.summary: Opens a directory via open/openat syscall with O_RDONLY, handling architecture-specific O_DIRECTORY flag availability (notably skipped on RISC-V due to QEMU translation issues).

nodes.name: DirectoryIterator::Next [POSIX]
nodes.summary: Advances to the next directory entry using buffered getdents64/getdirentries64 syscalls, then populates DirectoryEntry metadata via fstatat with architecture-specific stat struct offset tables spanning 8 platform/architecture combinations.

nodes.name: posix/file.cc
nodes.summary: POSIX implementation of the File class using open/openat, read, write, lseek, unlink, and stat syscalls with architecture-specific lseek wrappers for riscv32 (llseek) and FreeBSD i386 (split 64-bit offset).

nodes.name: File::Open [POSIX]
nodes.summary: Opens a file by converting wide path to UTF-8, translating mode flags to POSIX open flags, calling open/openat syscall, and querying file size via lseek(END)+lseek(SET).

nodes.name: PosixLseek
nodes.summary: Architecture-dispatched lseek wrapper handling riscv32's 5-argument llseek, FreeBSD i386's split 64-bit offset, and the standard 3-argument lseek for all other platforms.

nodes.name: posix_path.cc
nodes.summary: Implements NormalizePathToUtf8 which converts a wide-character path to a null-terminated UTF-8 string via Path::NormalizePath and UTF16::ToUTF8 for POSIX syscall consumption.

nodes.name: NormalizePathToUtf8
nodes.summary: Normalizes a wide-character path (separator conversion) and converts to null-terminated UTF-8 for use with POSIX filesystem syscalls.

nodes.name: posix_path.h
nodes.summary: Declares the NormalizePathToUtf8 helper function that converts wide-character paths to UTF-8 null-terminated strings for POSIX syscalls.

nodes.name: uefi/directory.cc

nodes.name: DirectoryIterator::Next [UEFI]
nodes.summary: Reads the next directory entry from EFI_FILE_PROTOCOL into a 512-byte stack buffer, parsing EFI_FILE_INFO for filename, size, and attribute flags (directory, hidden, system, read-only).

nodes.name: uefi/file.cc
nodes.summary: UEFI implementation of the File class using EFI_FILE_PROTOCOL for open, read, write, seek (GetPosition/SetPosition), delete, and exists operations, with stack-built GUIDs for EFI_FILE_INFO queries and truncation support.

nodes.name: File::Open [UEFI]
nodes.summary: Opens a file via EFI_FILE_PROTOCOL with mode flag translation, optional truncation via SetInfo, and file size query via GetInfo before returning the RAII File wrapper.

nodes.name: File::MoveOffset [UEFI]
nodes.summary: Implements relative seek on UEFI by computing absolute position from current/start/end origin using GetPosition, then calling SetPosition.

nodes.name: uefi_fs_helpers.cc

nodes.name: GetRootDirectory

nodes.name: OpenFileFromRoot
nodes.summary: Opens a file by normalizing the path separators to backslashes and calling EFI_FILE_PROTOCOL::Open from a root directory handle.

nodes.name: uefi_fs_helpers.h
nodes.summary: Declares UEFI filesystem helper functions: MakeFsProtocolGuid (stack-built GUID without .rdata), GetRootDirectory (volume root handle), and OpenFileFromRoot (path-based file opening).

nodes.name: windows/directory.cc

nodes.name: Directory::Delete [Windows]
nodes.summary: Deletes a directory by opening with DELETE access via ZwOpenFile then setting FileDispositionInformation via ZwSetInformationFile.

nodes.name: directory_iterator.cc

nodes.name: DirectoryIterator::Next

nodes.name: file.cc

nodes.name: File::Open
nodes.summary: Factory that opens a file via ZwCreateFile with configurable read/write/create/truncate/append flags, converting DOS paths to NT paths.

nodes.name: File::Delete
nodes.summary: Deletes a file by opening it with FILE_DELETE_ON_CLOSE and immediately closing the handle.

nodes.name: File::MoveOffset
nodes.summary: Repositions the file pointer relative to start, current position, or end using ZwQueryInformationFile and ZwSetInformationFile.

nodes.name: GetDyldAllImageInfos

nodes.name: ResolveDyldFunctions

nodes.name: ResolveFrameworkFunction
nodes.summary: Public API that resolves a named function from a macOS framework by first resolving dlopen/dlsym from dyld, then loading the framework and looking up the function.

nodes.name: dyld.h

nodes.name: mach.h

nodes.name: MachMsgTrap

nodes.name: system.aarch64.h (macos)

nodes.name: System (aarch64 macos)

nodes.name: system.h (macos)

nodes.name: system.x86_64.h (macos)

nodes.name: System (x86_64 macos)
nodes.summary: Provides 7 static NOINLINE Call() overloads for invoking macOS BSD syscalls on x86_64 via syscall instruction, with carry-flag negation and rdx clobber.

nodes.name: platform_result.h (solaris)
nodes.summary: Provides the FromSolaris() template helper that converts raw Solaris syscall return values into Result<T, Error>, mapping negative values to POSIX error codes after carry-flag normalization.

nodes.name: syscall.h (solaris)
nodes.summary: Defines Oracle Solaris 11.4 syscall numbers, POSIX constants, file/socket/memory flags, errno values, and kernel structures (SolarisUtsname, SolarisDirent64, Timespec, Pollfd) with platform-specific values differing from Linux.

nodes.name: system.aarch64.h (solaris)
nodes.summary: AArch64 Solaris syscall implementation providing System::Call overloads (0-6 arguments) using svc #0 with syscall number in x8, carry-flag error normalization via negation.

nodes.name: System (aarch64 solaris)
nodes.summary: Provides 7 static NOINLINE Call() overloads for invoking Solaris syscalls on AArch64 via svc #0, with carry-flag negation and x1 clobber.

nodes.name: system.h (solaris)
nodes.summary: Architecture-selection dispatcher that includes the correct arch-specific System::Call implementation for Solaris (x86_64, i386, or AArch64).

nodes.name: system.i386.h (solaris)
nodes.summary: i386 Solaris syscall implementation providing System::Call overloads (0-6 arguments) using int $0x91 trap gate with stack-based argument passing and carry-flag error normalization.

nodes.name: System (i386 solaris)
nodes.summary: Provides 7 static NOINLINE Call() overloads for invoking Solaris syscalls on i386 via int $0x91, pushing arguments onto the stack with a dummy return address.

nodes.name: system.x86_64.h (solaris)
nodes.summary: x86_64 Solaris syscall implementation providing System::Call overloads (0-6 arguments) using the syscall instruction with carry-flag error normalization and rdx clobber for rval[1].

nodes.name: System (x86_64 solaris)
nodes.summary: Provides 7 static NOINLINE Call() overloads for invoking Solaris syscalls on x86_64 via syscall instruction, with carry-flag negation and rdx clobber.

nodes.name: efi_boot_services.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_BOOT_SERVICES function table with all associated typedefs for memory allocation, event/timer management, protocol handlers, image services, and miscellaneous utilities available before ExitBootServices().

nodes.name: efi_context.aarch64.h
nodes.summary: Provides AArch64 SetEfiContextRegister() and GetEfiContext() using the TPIDR_EL0 thread pointer register for fast per-CPU EFI_CONTEXT storage.

nodes.name: efi_context.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_CONTEXT structure (ImageHandle, SystemTable, network state flags) and dispatches to arch-specific context register accessors (GS base MSR for x86_64, TPIDR_EL0 for AArch64).

nodes.name: efi_context.x86_64.h
nodes.summary: Provides x86_64 SetEfiContextRegister() and GetEfiContext() using the IA32_GS_BASE MSR (0xC0000101) via WRMSR/RDMSR for ring-0 EFI_CONTEXT storage.

nodes.name: efi_file_protocol.h
nodes.summary: Defines EFI_FILE_PROTOCOL for UEFI file I/O (open, close, read, write, position, info, flush), EFI_SIMPLE_FILE_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL for volume access, EFI_FILE_INFO metadata structure, and file attribute/mode constants.

nodes.name: efi_graphics_output_protocol.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI Graphics Output Protocol (GOP) interface for display mode queries and framebuffer BLT operations, including pixel format enums, mode info structures, and BLT pixel types.

nodes.name: efi_ip4_config2_protocol.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_IP4_CONFIG2_PROTOCOL for IPv4 network configuration supporting static and DHCP address assignment, with data type enums, manual address structs, and interface info queries.

nodes.name: efi_protocols.h
nodes.summary: Defines EFI_SIMPLE_TEXT_OUTPUT_PROTOCOL for text console output (OutputString, ClearScreen, SetAttribute) and EFI_SIMPLE_TEXT_INPUT_PROTOCOL for keyboard input (ReadKeyStroke), with text color constants.

nodes.name: efi_runtime_services.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_RUNTIME_SERVICES table with time services, variable services, virtual memory mapping, system reset, capsule update, and associated structures (EFI_TIME, EFI_RESET_TYPE, EFI_CAPSULE_HEADER).

nodes.name: efi_service_binding.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_SERVICE_BINDING_PROTOCOL with CreateChild and DestroyChild functions used by UEFI network protocol drivers for per-connection handle management.

nodes.name: efi_simple_network_protocol.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_SIMPLE_NETWORK_PROTOCOL for low-level NIC operations (Start, Stop, Initialize, Transmit, Receive) and EFI_SIMPLE_NETWORK_MODE for querying NIC capabilities and MAC addresses.

nodes.name: efi_system_table.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_SYSTEM_TABLE primary entry structure providing access to console protocols, Boot Services, Runtime Services, and configuration tables (ACPI, SMBIOS GUIDs).

nodes.name: efi_tcp4_protocol.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_TCP4_PROTOCOL interface for TCP/IPv4 communication in UEFI including configuration, connection state, async I/O tokens, fragment data, and the full protocol function table (Connect, Accept, Transmit, Receive, Close).

nodes.name: efi_tcp6_protocol.h
nodes.summary: Defines the EFI_TCP6_PROTOCOL interface and all supporting types for TCP/IPv6 communication in UEFI, including configuration structures, connection state enumeration, async I/O tokens, fragment data, and the protocol function table.

nodes.name: efi_types.h
nodes.summary: Defines fundamental UEFI data types (EFI_STATUS, EFI_HANDLE, EFI_GUID), memory type enumerations, status code macros, task priority levels, EFIAPI calling convention, and the EFI_TABLE_HEADER common to all UEFI system tables.

nodes.name: platform_result.h (UEFI)
nodes.summary: Provides the result::FromEfiStatus() template helper that converts UEFI EFI_STATUS codes into Result<T, Error> for uniform cross-platform error handling.

nodes.name: result::FromEfiStatus<T>
nodes.summary: Template function that converts a UEFI EFI_STATUS code to Result<T, Error>, treating non-negative values as success and negative (high-bit-set) values as errors.

nodes.name: gdi32.cc
nodes.summary: Implements position-independent wrappers for gdi32.dll GDI functions (CreateCompatibleDC, CreateCompatibleBitmap, SelectObject, BitBlt, GetDIBits, DeleteDC, DeleteObject) using DJB2 hash-based runtime export resolution.

nodes.name: gdi32.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Gdi32 class with static methods wrapping Win32 GDI functions from gdi32.dll, plus BITMAPINFOHEADER struct and GDI constants (SRCCOPY, BI_RGB, DIB_RGB_COLORS).

nodes.name: Gdi32
nodes.summary: Static class providing position-independent access to gdi32.dll exports for bitmap creation, device context management, and pixel data extraction, all resolved dynamically via DJB2 hash-based PEB lookup.

nodes.name: kernel32.cc
nodes.summary: Implements position-independent wrappers for kernel32.dll functions (CreateProcessW, SetHandleInformation, CreatePipe, PeekNamedPipe) using DJB2 hash-based runtime export resolution.

nodes.name: kernel32.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Kernel32 class with static methods wrapping Win32 API functions from kernel32.dll for process creation and handle/pipe management, plus STARTUPINFOW and PROCESS_INFORMATION structures.

nodes.name: Kernel32
nodes.summary: Static class providing position-independent access to kernel32.dll exports for process creation (CreateProcessW), handle management (SetHandleInformation), and pipe operations (CreatePipe, PeekNamedPipe).

nodes.name: ntdll.cc
nodes.summary: Implements all NTDLL class wrappers for NT Native API Zw*/Rtl* functions using indirect syscalls (via System::Call with resolved SSNs) or direct ntdll export calls, covering file I/O, memory, process, pipe, and DLL loading operations.

nodes.name: ntdll.h
nodes.summary: Declares the NTDLL class with 25+ static methods wrapping NT Native API functions (Zw* syscalls and Rtl* runtime routines), plus supporting NT structures (FILE_BASIC_INFORMATION, FILE_BOTH_DIR_INFORMATION, RTL_RELATIVE_NAME_U, etc.) and enumerations.

nodes.name: NTDLL
nodes.summary: Static class providing the lowest-level OS interface on Windows through position-independent wrappers around ntdll.dll exports, using indirect syscalls on x86_64/i386 and direct ntdll calls on ARM, with Result<NTSTATUS, Error> return types.

nodes.name: peb.cc
nodes.summary: Implements PEB access functions: GetCurrentPEB (arch-specific TEB register reads), GetModuleHandleFromPEB (walks InMemoryOrderModuleList with DJB2 hash matching), and ResolveExportAddress/ResolveExportAddressFromPebModule for dynamic function resolution.

nodes.name: GetCurrentPEB
nodes.summary: Retrieves the current process's PEB pointer using architecture-specific inline assembly to read from GS:[0x60] (x86_64), FS:[0x30] (i386), or TEB register offsets (ARM64/ARM32).

nodes.name: GetModuleHandleFromPEB
nodes.summary: Walks the PEB's InMemoryOrderModuleList comparing each module's BaseDllName DJB2 hash against the target to resolve a module's base address without Win32 API calls.

nodes.name: ResolveExportAddressFromPebModule
nodes.summary: Resolves an exported function address by first finding the module via PEB walk using DJB2 hash of module name, then parsing its PE export directory.

nodes.name: ResolveExportAddress
nodes.summary: Resolves an exported function with a fallback path that uses NTDLL::LdrLoadDll to load the module if it is not already present in the PEB module list.

nodes.name: peb.h
nodes.summary: Defines Windows PEB and loader data structures (PEB, PEB_LDR_DATA, LDR_DATA_TABLE_ENTRY, LIST_ENTRY, RTL_USER_PROCESS_PARAMETERS) and declares functions for PEB access and DJB2-based module/export resolution.

nodes.name: pe.cc
nodes.summary: Implements GetExportAddress which parses a PE module's DOS/NT headers and export directory at runtime, resolving exported functions by DJB2 name hash with support for forwarded exports.

nodes.name: GetExportAddress
nodes.summary: Parses a loaded PE module's export directory to resolve a function by DJB2 hash of its name, handling both direct exports and forwarded exports by recursively resolving through the target module.

nodes.name: pe.h
nodes.summary: Defines Windows PE format structures (IMAGE_DOS_HEADER, IMAGE_NT_HEADERS32/64, IMAGE_FILE_HEADER, IMAGE_OPTIONAL_HEADER32/64, IMAGE_DATA_DIRECTORY, IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY) used for runtime PE parsing and export resolution.

nodes.name: platform_result.h (Windows)
nodes.summary: Provides the result::FromNTSTATUS() template helper that converts Windows NTSTATUS codes into Result<T, Error> using NT_SUCCESS semantics (status >= 0 indicates success).

nodes.name: result::FromNTSTATUS<T>
nodes.summary: Template function that converts a Windows NTSTATUS to Result<T, Error>, treating non-negative values as success and negative values as Error::Windows wrapped errors.

nodes.name: system.aarch64.h
nodes.summary: Provides System::Call overloads (0-14 arguments) for AArch64 Windows indirect syscalls using BLR to ntdll stub addresses, with args in x0-x7 and overflow on stack, x16 holding the stub address.

nodes.name: system.armv7a.h
nodes.summary: Provides System::Call overloads (0-14 arguments) for ARMv7-A Windows indirect syscalls using BLX to ntdll stub addresses, with args in r0-r3 and overflow on stack, r12 holding the stub address.

nodes.name: system.cc
nodes.summary: Implements System::ResolveSyscallEntry which scans ntdll's export table for Zw* functions, extracts System Service Numbers from function prologues, and locates syscall/sysenter gadgets for indirect syscall dispatch across x86_64, i386, ARM64, and ARM32.

nodes.name: System::ResolveSyscallEntry
nodes.summary: Resolves a SYSCALL_ENTRY (SSN + gadget address) for a Zw* function by scanning ntdll exports, extracting SSNs from function prologues, and locating architecture-specific syscall gadgets (0F05C3 on x64, SVC+RET on ARM64, SVC#1 on ARM32, KiFastSystemCall on i386).

nodes.name: system.h
nodes.summary: Declares the System class for indirect syscall dispatch on Windows, the SYSCALL_ENTRY struct, ResolveSyscall macro, and conditionally includes architecture-specific Call overload headers (x86_64, i386, aarch64, armv7a).

nodes.name: System
nodes.summary: Provides indirect system call dispatch for Windows NT via architecture-specific inline assembly, resolving SSNs at runtime and dispatching through ntdll gadgets to bypass userland API hooks.

nodes.name: system.i386.h
nodes.summary: Provides System::Call overloads (0-14 arguments) for i386 Windows indirect syscalls with all args pushed on stack, EAX holding SSN, and EDX pointing to KiFastSystemCall or WoW64 trampoline gadget.

nodes.name: system.x86_64.h
nodes.summary: Provides System::Call overloads (0-14 arguments) for x86_64 Windows indirect syscalls with RCX->R10 swap, args in R10/RDX/R8/R9 + stack shadow space, RAX holding SSN, and indirect call to ntdll syscall gadget.

nodes.name: user32.cc
nodes.summary: Implements position-independent wrappers for user32.dll functions (EnumDisplayDevicesW, EnumDisplaySettingsW, GetDC, ReleaseDC) using DJB2 hash-based runtime export resolution.

nodes.name: user32.h
nodes.summary: Declares the User32 class with static methods wrapping user32.dll exports for display enumeration and device context management, plus DISPLAY_DEVICEW and DEVMODEW structures.

nodes.name: User32
nodes.summary: Static class providing position-independent access to user32.dll exports for display device enumeration and screen device context acquisition.

nodes.name: windows_types.h
nodes.summary: Defines core Windows NT types (NTSTATUS, UNICODE_STRING, OBJECT_ATTRIBUTES, IO_STATUS_BLOCK, LARGE_INTEGER), file/memory/device constants, SMBIOS structures, and the InitializeObjectAttributes macro, replacing Windows SDK headers.

nodes.name: allocator.cc
nodes.summary: Overrides global operator new/delete to route all heap allocations through the platform-specific Allocator, eliminating CRT dependencies.

nodes.name: allocator.h
nodes.summary: Declares the Allocator class with static AllocateMemory/ReleaseMemory methods that provide platform-specific virtual memory allocation via direct syscalls (NtAllocateVirtualMemory, mmap, or AllocatePool).

nodes.name: Allocator
nodes.summary: Cross-platform memory allocator providing AllocateMemory and ReleaseMemory via platform-specific syscalls (NtAllocateVirtualMemory on Windows, mmap/munmap on POSIX, AllocatePool/FreePool on UEFI).

nodes.name: memory.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: Implements Allocator::AllocateMemory/ReleaseMemory using mmap/munmap syscalls with a prepended USIZE header for size tracking, supporting Linux, Android, macOS, iOS, Solaris, and FreeBSD including special handling for 32-bit and FreeBSD i386.

nodes.name: memory.cc (UEFI)
nodes.summary: Implements Allocator::AllocateMemory/ReleaseMemory using UEFI Boot Services AllocatePool/FreePool with EfiLoaderData memory type.

nodes.name: memory.cc (Windows)
nodes.summary: Implements Allocator::AllocateMemory/ReleaseMemory using NTDLL::ZwAllocateVirtualMemory and ZwFreeVirtualMemory with MEM_COMMIT|MEM_RESERVE and PAGE_READWRITE.

nodes.name: platform.h
nodes.summary: Platform Abstraction Layer aggregate header that conditionally includes all platform-specific headers and pulls in memory allocation, system utilities, I/O services, networking, display, and process management subsystems.

nodes.name: screen.cc (iOS)
nodes.summary: Stub implementation of Screen::GetDevices and Screen::Capture for iOS that returns error results, since iOS screen capture requires UIKit/Objective-C runtime which is inaccessible from position-independent code.

nodes.name: screen.cc (macOS)
nodes.summary: macOS screen capture implementation using CoreGraphics framework, dynamically resolved at runtime via dyld. Enumerates displays with CGGetActiveDisplayList and captures framebuffers with CGDisplayCreateImage, using a forked child process to probe display availability safely.

nodes.name: Screen::GetDevices
nodes.summary: Probes display availability via forked child, loads CoreGraphics functions, and enumerates up to 16 active displays with bounds and primary status.

nodes.name: Screen::Capture
nodes.summary: Captures a macOS display by matching device geometry, using CGDisplayCreateImage and CGDataProviderCopyData, then converting BGRA/RGBA pixel data to RGB.

nodes.name: MacFork
nodes.summary: Custom fork wrapper using inline assembly to correctly distinguish parent/child on macOS by checking the secondary return value (rdx/x1).

nodes.name: LoadCGFunctions
nodes.summary: Dynamically resolves 15 CoreGraphics/CoreFoundation function pointers at runtime using dyld framework resolver, returning true only if all resolve successfully.

nodes.name: screen.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: Large POSIX screen implementation (2483 lines) supporting three capture backends: X11 raw wire protocol over Unix sockets, DRM dumb buffers via KMS ioctls, and Linux/Solaris framebuffer devices. Also supports Android screencap fallback.

nodes.name: screen.h
nodes.summary: Cross-platform screen device enumeration and capture header defining ScreenDevice struct, ScreenDeviceList result container, and the static Screen class with GetDevices() and Capture() methods.

nodes.name: Screen
nodes.summary: Static, stack-only class providing cross-platform screen device enumeration (GetDevices) and framebuffer capture (Capture) with platform-specific backends for Windows, Linux, macOS, UEFI, and Solaris.

nodes.name: screen.cc (UEFI)
nodes.summary: UEFI screen implementation using EFI Graphics Output Protocol (GOP). GetDevices reports the current video mode as a single display; Capture uses GOP Blt() to copy framebuffer contents to RGB.

nodes.name: screen.cc (Windows)
nodes.summary: Windows screen implementation using User32 EnumDisplayDevicesW/EnumDisplaySettingsW for enumeration and GDI CreateCompatibleDC/BitBlt/GetDIBits for BGRA framebuffer capture with conversion to RGB.

nodes.name: socket.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: Unified POSIX TCP socket implementation for Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, and Solaris with platform-specific syscall helpers for socket, connect, send, recv, fcntl, and poll operations. Handles Linux i386 socketcall multiplexing and Solaris so_socket.

nodes.name: Socket::Create
nodes.summary: Creates a TCP stream socket via PosixSocket syscall helper, returning a Socket object with the file descriptor as handle.

nodes.name: Socket::Open
nodes.summary: Connects the socket using non-blocking connect with 5-second poll timeout, checking SO_ERROR on completion and restoring blocking mode.

nodes.name: socket.h
nodes.summary: Cross-platform TCP stream socket header defining SockAddr/SockAddr6 structures, SocketAddressHelper for IPv4/IPv6 address preparation, and the RAII Socket class with Create/Open/Close/Read/Write lifecycle methods.

nodes.name: Socket
nodes.summary: RAII TCP stream socket supporting IPv4/IPv6 with factory Create(), three-way handshake Open(), data transfer Read/Write, and platform backends for Windows AFD, POSIX BSD sockets, and UEFI TCP protocols.

nodes.name: SocketAddressHelper
nodes.summary: Utility class that converts IPAddress objects to SockAddr/SockAddr6 structures, handling IPv4/IPv6 dispatch, byte-order conversion, and BSD-specific sockaddr_in length fields.

nodes.name: socket.cc (UEFI)
nodes.summary: UEFI TCP socket implementation (931 lines) using EFI_TCP4_PROTOCOL/EFI_TCP6_PROTOCOL via Service Binding. Handles network interface initialization (SNP start/initialize, IP4 config wait), connection with polling, and chunked transmit/receive with completion events.

nodes.name: socket.cc (Windows)
nodes.summary: Windows TCP socket implementation using AFD (Auxiliary Function Driver) IOCTLs via NT Native API. Opens \Device\Afd\Endpoint with ZwCreateFile, performs bind/connect/send/recv through IOCTL_AFD_* with event-based async completion and timeouts.

nodes.name: Socket::Create
nodes.summary: Creates a Windows TCP socket by opening \Device\Afd\Endpoint via ZwCreateFile with AfdOpenPacketXX extended attributes specifying address family, socket type, and protocol.

nodes.name: platform.cc (Android)
nodes.summary: Minimal Android platform file providing ExitProcess via SYS_EXIT syscall with __builtin_unreachable.

nodes.name: date_time.h
nodes.summary: DateTime class header providing system clock access via Now(), monotonic timestamps via GetMonotonicNanoseconds(), constexpr date/time formatting to fixed-size stack strings (FixedString), and calendar helpers for leap year and epoch-to-date conversion.

nodes.name: DateTime
nodes.summary: Date/time representation with constexpr formatting methods (ToTimeOnlyString, ToDateOnlyString, ToDateTimeString), static Now() for wall-clock retrieval, GetMonotonicNanoseconds(), and shared calendar helpers (IsLeapYear, FromDaysAndTime).

nodes.name: environment.h
nodes.summary: Header for Environment class providing position-independent access to environment variables (GetVariable), compile-time platform and architecture identification, runtime OS version, and hostname retrieval across all supported targets.

nodes.name: Environment
nodes.summary: Static class for environment variable access (via PEB on Windows, /proc/self/environ on Linux) and platform identification (GetAgentPlatform, GetOSVersion, GetHostname, GetArchitecture).

nodes.name: platform.cc (FreeBSD)
nodes.summary: Minimal FreeBSD platform file providing ExitProcess via SYS_EXIT syscall with __builtin_unreachable.

nodes.name: platform.cc (iOS)
nodes.summary: iOS platform file providing ExitProcess via SYS_EXIT syscall and a no-op dyld_stub_binder export to satisfy the linker requirement when building with -nostdlib on ARM64.

nodes.name: platform.cc (Linux)
nodes.summary: Minimal Linux platform file providing ExitProcess via SYS_EXIT syscall with __builtin_unreachable.

nodes.name: machine_id.h
nodes.summary: Header declaring GetMachineUUID() which retrieves a stable machine-unique UUID from platform-specific sources: SMBIOS on Windows, /etc/machine-id or boot_id on Linux, kern.uuid sysctl on macOS.

nodes.name: platform.cc (macOS)
nodes.summary: macOS platform file providing ExitProcess via SYS_EXIT syscall, plus a conditional dyld_stub_binder no-op export for ARM64 -nostdlib builds.

nodes.name: pipe.h
nodes.summary: Header for Pipe class providing RAII anonymous pipe abstraction with Create factory, Read/Write, per-end CloseRead/CloseWrite, and move-only semantics. Used for inter-process I/O redirection.

nodes.name: Pipe
nodes.summary: RAII wrapper for an anonymous pipe (read/write fd pair) with static Create factory, move-only ownership, and per-end close operations for POSIX and Windows backends.

nodes.name: date_time.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX DateTime::Now() implementation using clock_gettime (Linux/Solaris/FreeBSD) or gettimeofday (macOS/iOS) to retrieve wall-clock time, and GetMonotonicNanoseconds using CLOCK_MONOTONIC or gettimeofday fallback.

nodes.name: DateTime::Now
nodes.summary: Retrieves current wall-clock time via platform-specific syscall (clock_gettime or gettimeofday), converts Unix timestamp to DateTime using FromDaysAndTime helper.

nodes.name: environment.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX Environment implementation: GetVariable reads /proc/self/environ on Linux/Android (stub on other POSIX); GetAgentPlatform/GetArchitecture return compile-time strings; GetOSVersion uses uname/sysctl/utssys; GetHostname uses env vars, sysctl, or /etc/hostname.

nodes.name: Environment::GetVariable
nodes.summary: Reads environment variables from /proc/self/environ on Linux/Android by opening the procfs file and scanning null-separated entries; returns stub (0) on macOS/FreeBSD/Solaris.

nodes.name: Environment::GetOSVersion
nodes.summary: Retrieves runtime OS version string using uname syscall (Linux/Android), sysctl kern.ostype+kern.osrelease (macOS/FreeBSD), or utssys with /etc/release fallback (Solaris).

nodes.name: machine_id.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX GetMachineUUID implementation reading /etc/machine-id (systemd hex format) or /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id (UUID format) on Linux, and using sysctl kern.uuid name2oid resolution on macOS/iOS.

nodes.name: GetMachineUUID
nodes.summary: Retrieves machine UUID by trying /etc/machine-id, then /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id on Linux, or sysctl kern.uuid via name2oid on macOS/iOS.

nodes.name: pipe.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX Pipe implementation using pipe()/pipe2() syscall for creation and standard read/write/close syscalls for I/O, with architecture-specific handling for aarch64/riscv (pipe2) and Solaris.

nodes.name: process.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX Process implementation with fork+execve for creation, wait4/waitid for lifecycle management, and kill for termination. Handles BSD fork rval[1] convention on macOS/iOS/Solaris via custom inline assembly, plus dup2/setsid/TIOCSCTTY for I/O redirection and PTY control.

nodes.name: PosixFork
nodes.summary: Platform-aware fork using custom inline assembly on macOS/iOS/Solaris to handle BSD rval[1] convention (rdx/x1 distinguishes parent from child), clone(SIGCHLD) on Linux aarch64/riscv, or standard SYS_FORK elsewhere.

nodes.name: Process::Create
nodes.summary: Spawns a child process via PosixFork, with optional stdin/stdout/stderr redirection using dup2, setsid/TIOCSCTTY for controlling terminal, and execve to replace the child image.

nodes.name: Process::Wait
nodes.summary: Blocks until child process exits using wait4 (most platforms) or waitid (Solaris, Linux riscv32), extracting the exit code from status or siginfo_t at platform-specific offsets.

nodes.name: pty.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX pseudo-terminal implementation with platform-specific master opening (/dev/ptmx or posix_openpt on FreeBSD), unlock (TIOCSPTLCK on Linux, STREAMS I_STR on Solaris, TIOCPTYUNLK on macOS), slave path discovery, and read/write/poll operations.

nodes.name: PtyOpenPair
nodes.summary: Opens a PTY master/slave pair with platform-specific logic: /dev/ptmx + TIOCSPTLCK/TIOCGPTN on Linux, posix_openpt + FIODGNAME on FreeBSD, STREAMS OWNERPT/UNLKPT + fstatat on Solaris, TIOCPTYGRANT/TIOCPTYUNLK/TIOCPTYGNAME on macOS.

nodes.name: shell_process.cc (POSIX)
nodes.summary: POSIX ShellProcess implementation spawning /bin/sh via PTY. Create allocates a PTY, forks a shell with all I/O on the slave fd, then provides Read/Write/Poll through the PTY master. ReadError returns 0 since PTY merges stdout/stderr.

nodes.name: ShellProcess::Create
nodes.summary: Creates a PTY, spawns /bin/sh with stdin/stdout/stderr redirected to the slave fd, closes the slave in the parent, and returns a ShellProcess wrapping the Process and PTY.

nodes.name: process.h
nodes.summary: Header for Process class providing RAII process handle with static Create factory, Wait, Terminate, IsRunning, and Close methods. Supports optional I/O redirection via fd parameters and move-only ownership semantics.

nodes.name: Process
nodes.summary: RAII wrapper for a spawned child process with fork+execve (POSIX) or CreateProcessW (Windows) backends, providing Create, Wait, Terminate, IsRunning, and optional I/O redirection.

nodes.name: pty.h
nodes.summary: Header for Pty class providing RAII pseudo-terminal abstraction with Create factory, Read/Write/Poll on master fd, SlaveFd accessor for Process I/O redirection, and CloseSlave for parent-side cleanup.

nodes.name: Pty
nodes.summary: RAII wrapper for a pseudo-terminal master/slave pair with static Create factory, master-side Read/Write/Poll, slave fd access for child process redirection, and move-only ownership.

nodes.name: random.cc

nodes.name: emitPCRelativeFuncRef

nodes.name: replaceGlobalInFunction

nodes.name: materializeConstant
